title: 10.1 GENERAL

data.rule: 10.1 GENERAL

title: Atmospheric ice loads caused by freezing rain, snow, and in-cloud icing shall be considered in the design of ice-sensitive structures. In areas where records or experience indicate that snow or in-clo

data.rule: Atmospheric ice loads caused by freezing rain, snow, and in-cloud icing shall be considered in the design of ice-sensitive structures. In areas where records or experience indicate that snow or in-cloud icing produces larger loads than freezing rain, site-speci fi c studies shall be used. Structural loads caused by hoarfrost are not a design consideration. Roof snow loads are covered in Chapter 7.

title: 10.1.1 Site-Speci fi c Studies Site-speci fi c studies that are acceptable to the Authority Having Jurisdiction shall be used to determine the ice thickness or load for the applicable Risk Category, c

data.rule: 10.1.1 Site-Speci fi c Studies Site-speci fi c studies that are acceptable to the Authority Having Jurisdiction shall be used to determine the ice thickness or load for the applicable Risk Category, concurrent wind speed, and concurrent temperature in

title: Areas where records or experience indicate that snow or in-cloud icing produce larger loads than freezing rain;

data.rule: Areas where records or experience indicate that snow or in-cloud icing produce larger loads than freezing rain;

title: Special icing regions, as shown in Figures 10.4-2 through 10.4-5; and

data.rule: Special icing regions, as shown in Figures 10.4-2 through 10.4-5; and

title: Complex terrain, where examination indicates unusual icing conditions exist.

data.rule: Complex terrain, where examination indicates unusual icing conditions exist.

title: 10.1.2 Dynamic Loads Dynamic loads, such as those resulting from galloping, ice shedding, and aeolian vibrations, which are caused, or enhanced, by an ice accretion on a fl exible structural member, c

data.rule: 10.1.2 Dynamic Loads Dynamic loads, such as those resulting from galloping, ice shedding, and aeolian vibrations, which are caused, or enhanced, by an ice accretion on a fl exible structural member, component, or appurtenance, are not covered in this section.

title: 10.1.3 Exclusions Electrical supply and communication systems, communications towers and masts, and other structures for which national standards exist are excluded from the requirements of this secti

data.rule: 10.1.3 Exclusions Electrical supply and communication systems, communications towers and masts, and other structures for which national standards exist are excluded from the requirements of this section. Applicable standards and documents include the National Electrical Safety Code, ASCE Manual of Practice 74, and ANSI/TIA-222.

title: 10.2 DEFINITIONS

data.rule: 10.2 DEFINITIONS

title: The following de fi nitions apply only to the provisions of this chapter.

data.rule: The following de fi nitions apply only to the provisions of this chapter.

title: ATMOSPHERIC ICE GEODATABASE: The ASCE database (version 2022-1.0) of geocoded nominal ice thickness, concurrent wind, and concurrent temperature data.

data.definition: The ASCE database (version 2022-1.0) of geocoded nominal ice thickness, concurrent wind, and concurrent temperature data.

title: COMPONENTS AND APPURTENANCES: Nonstructural elements that may be exposed to atmospheric icing. Examples include ladders, handrails, netting, antennas, waveguides, radio frequency (RF) transmission lin

data.definition: Nonstructural elements that may be exposed to atmospheric icing. Examples include ladders, handrails, netting, antennas, waveguides, radio frequency (RF) transmission lines, pipes, electrical conduits, and cable trays.

title: FREEZING RAIN: Rain or drizzle that falls into a layer of subfreezing air at the Earth ' s surface and freezes on the ground or an object to form glaze ice.

data.definition: Rain or drizzle that falls into a layer of subfreezing air at the Earth ' s surface and freezes on the ground or an object to form glaze ice.

title: GLAZE: Clear, high-density ice.
CHAPTER 10 ICE LOADS -ATMOSPHERIC ICING

data.rule: CHAPTER 10 ICE LOADS -ATMOSPHERIC ICING

title: HOARFROST: An accumulation of ice crystals formed by the direct deposition of water vapor from the air onto an object.

data.definition: An accumulation of ice crystals formed by the direct deposition of water vapor from the air onto an object.

title: ICE-SENSITIVE STRUCTURES: Structures for which the effect of an atmospheric icing load governs the design of part, or all, of the structure. These structures include, but are not limited to, lattice s

data.rule: ICE-SENSITIVE STRUCTURES: Structures for which the effect of an atmospheric icing load governs the design of part, or all, of the structure. These structures include, but are not limited to, lattice structures, guyed masts, overhead electric and communication lines, light suspension and cable-stayed bridges, aerial cable systems (e.g., for ski lifts and logging operations), amusement rides, open catwalks and platforms, fl agpoles, and signs.

title: IN-CLOUD ICING: Icing that occurs when supercooled cloud or fog droplets, carried by the wind, freeze on objects. In-cloud icing usually forms rime but may also form glaze.

data.rule: IN-CLOUD ICING: Icing that occurs when supercooled cloud or fog droplets, carried by the wind, freeze on objects. In-cloud icing usually forms rime but may also form glaze.

title: RIME: White or opaque ice with entrapped air.

data.definition: White or opaque ice with entrapped air.

title: SNOW: Snow that adheres to objects by some combination of capillary forces, freezing, and sintering.

data.definition: Snow that adheres to objects by some combination of capillary forces, freezing, and sintering.

title: 10.4.1 Ice Load The ice load shall be determined using the volume or cross-sectional area of glaze ice formed on all exposed surfaces of structural members, guys, components, appurtenances, and cable

data.rule: 10.4.1 Ice Load The ice load shall be determined using the volume or cross-sectional area of glaze ice formed on all exposed surfaces of structural members, guys, components, appurtenances, and cable systems. title: 10.4.4 Topographic Factor Both the ice thickness and concurrent gust speed for structures on hills, ridges, and escarpments are higher than those on level terrain because of wind speed-up effects. The

data.rule: 10.4.4 Topographic Factor Both the ice thickness and concurrent gust speed for structures on hills, ridges, and escarpments are higher than those on level terrain because of wind speed-up effects. The topographic factor for the concurrent wind pressure is Kzt , and the topographic factor for ice thickness is ð Kzt Þ 0.35 , where Kzt is obtained from Equation (26.8-1).

title: 10.4.5 Design Ice Thickness for Freezing Rain The design ice thickness t d shall be calculated from Equation (10.4-5).

data.rule: 10.4.5 Design Ice Thickness for Freezing Rain The design ice thickness t d shall be calculated from Equation (10.4-5).

title: 10.5 WIND ON ICE-COVERED STRUCTURES

data.rule: 10.5 WIND ON ICE-COVERED STRUCTURES

title: Ice accreted on structural members, components, and appurtenances increases the projected area of the structure exposed to wind. The projected area shall be increased by adding t d to all free edges o

data.rule: Ice accreted on structural members, components, and appurtenances increases the projected area of the structure exposed to wind. The projected area shall be increased by adding t d to all free edges of the projected area. Wind loads on this increased projected area shall be used in the design of ice-sensitive structures. Figures 10.5-1 and 10.5-2 show 3s gust speeds at 33 ft (10 m) above grade that are concurrent with the ice loads caused by freezing rain. These gust speeds shall be used with nominal ice thicknesses for all risk categories. Wind loads shall be calculated in accordance with Chapters 26 through 31, as modi fi ed by Sections 10.5.1 through 10.5.5.

title: 10.5.1 Wind on Ice-Covered Chimneys, Tanks, and Similar Structures Force coef fi cients, Cf , for structures with square, hexagonal, and octagonal cross sections shall be as given in Figure 29.4-1. Fo

data.rule: 10.5.1 Wind on Ice-Covered Chimneys, Tanks, and Similar Structures Force coef fi cients, Cf , for structures with square, hexagonal, and octagonal cross sections shall be as given in Figure 29.4-1. Force coef fi cients, Cf , for structures with round cross sections shall be as given in Figure 29.4-1 for the entry round, D p qz ≤ 2.5 for all ice thicknesses, wind speeds, and structure diameters.

title: 10.5.2 Wind on Ice-Covered Solid Freestanding Walls and Solid Signs Force coef fi cients, Cf , shall be as given in Figure 29.3-1, based on the dimensions of the wall or sign, including ice.

data.rule: 10.5.2 Wind on Ice-Covered Solid Freestanding Walls and Solid Signs Force coef fi cients, Cf , shall be as given in Figure 29.3-1, based on the dimensions of the wall or sign, including ice.

title: 10.5.3 Wind on Ice-Covered Open Signs and Lattice Frameworks The solidity ratio, ε , shall be based on the projected area, including ice. The force coef fi cient, Cf , for the projected area of fl at

data.rule: 10.5.3 Wind on Ice-Covered Open Signs and Lattice Frameworks The solidity ratio, ε , shall be based on the projected area, including ice. The force coef fi cient, Cf , for the projected area of fl at members shall be as given in Figure 29.4-2. The force coef fi cient, Cf , for rounded members and for the additional projected area caused by ice on both fl at and rounded members shall be as given in Figure 29.4-2 for rounded members with D p qz ≤ 2.5 for all ice thicknesses, wind speeds, and member diameters.

title: 10.5.4 Wind on Ice-Covered Trussed Towers The solidity ratio, ε , shall be based on the projected area, including ice. The force coef fi cients, Cf , shall be as given in Figure 29.4-3. It is acceptab

data.rule: 10.5.4 Wind on Ice-Covered Trussed Towers The solidity ratio, ε , shall be based on the projected area, including ice. The force coef fi cients, Cf , shall be as given in Figure 29.4-3. It is acceptable to reduce the force coef fi cients, Cf , for the additional projected area caused by ice on both round and fl at members by the factor for rounded members in Note 3 of Figure 29.4-3.

title: 10.5.5 Wind on Ice-Covered Guys and Cables The force coef fi cient, Cf , (as de fi ned in Chapter 29) for ice-covered guys and cables shall be 1.2.

data.rule: 10.5.5 Wind on Ice-Covered Guys and Cables The force coef fi cient, Cf , (as de fi ned in Chapter 29) for ice-covered guys and cables shall be 1.2.

title: 10.6 DESIGN TEMPERATURES FOR FREEZING RAIN

data.rule: 10.6 DESIGN TEMPERATURES FOR FREEZING RAIN

title: The design temperatures for ice, and wind-on-ice, caused by freezing rain shall be either the concurrent temperature for the site shown in Figures 10.6-1 and 10.6-2 or 32 °F (0° C), whichever gives th

data.rule: The design temperatures for ice, and wind-on-ice, caused by freezing rain shall be either the concurrent temperature for the site shown in Figures 10.6-1 and 10.6-2 or 32 °F (0° C), whichever gives the maximum load effect. The temperature for Hawaii shall be 32 °F (0 °C). For temperature-sensitive structures, the load shall include the effect of temperature change from everyday conditions to the design temperature for ice and wind on ice. These temperatures shall be used with nominal ice thicknesses for all Risk Categories.

title: 10.7 PARTIAL LOADING

data.rule: 10.7 PARTIAL LOADING

title: The full intensity of the ice load applied to a portion of a structure or member shall be accounted for when it produces a critical load effect. It is permitted to consider this to be a static load.

data.rule: The full intensity of the ice load applied to a portion of a structure or member shall be accounted for when it produces a critical load effect. It is permitted to consider this to be a static load.

title: 10.8 DESIGN PROCEDURE

data.rule: 10.8 DESIGN PROCEDURE

title: The nominal ice thickness, t , the concurrent wind speed, Vc , and the concurrent temperature for the site shall be determined from Figures 10.4-2 through 10.4-5, 10.5-1, 10.5-2, 10.6-1, and 10.6-2 or

data.rule: The nominal ice thickness, t , the concurrent wind speed, Vc , and the concurrent temperature for the site shall be determined from Figures 10.4-2 through 10.4-5, 10.5-1, 10.5-2, 10.6-1, and 10.6-2 or a sitespeci fi c study.

title: The topographic factor for the site, Kzt , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.4.4.

data.rule: The topographic factor for the site, Kzt , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.4.4.

title: The height factor, f z , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.4.3 for each design segment of the structure.

data.rule: The height factor, f z , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.4.3 for each design segment of the structure.

title: The design ice thickness, t d , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.4.5, Equation (10.4-5).

data.rule: The design ice thickness, t d , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.4.5, Equation (10.4-5).

title: The ice load shall be calculated for the design ice thickness, t d , in accordance with Section 10.4.1.

data.rule: The ice load shall be calculated for the design ice thickness, t d , in accordance with Section 10.4.1.

title: The velocity pressure, qz , for wind speed, Vc , shall be determined in accordance with Section 29.3.

data.rule: The velocity pressure, qz , for wind speed, Vc , shall be determined in accordance with Section 29.3.

title: The wind force coef fi cients, Cf , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.5.

data.rule: The wind force coef fi cients, Cf , shall be determined in accordance with Section 10.5.

title: The gust effect factor shall be determined in accordance with Section 26.11.

data.rule: The gust effect factor shall be determined in accordance with Section 26.11.

title: The design wind force shall be determined in accordance with Chapter 29.

data.rule: The design wind force shall be determined in accordance with Chapter 29.

title: The iced structure shall be analyzed for the load combinations in either Section 2.3 or 2.4.

data.rule: The iced structure shall be analyzed for the load combinations in either Section 2.3 or 2.4.
